free_text: Collective-model analysis of the inelastic scattering
of 53 and 33 MeV helions by 56Fe.
Angle digitizing error
Data digitizing error
Error from the graphic bars
The elastic and inelastic studies at
33.45+0.13 MeV used the facilities of the Birmingham
radial ridge cyclotron.
A 99.95% 56Fe enriched target was used with an areal
density of 1.03+-0.10 mg/cm2.
Fig.1
Details of experiment are given
The inelastic helion scattering data at
53.4+0.2 MeV was obtained with the Harwell variable
energy cyclotron.
The scattered reaction products were
detected in a dE-E telescope for mass separation,
consisting of a 300 mu-m surface-barrier detector
followed by a 3 mm Si(Li) detector cooled to -60 degr.
Beam drifts across the target were monitored by
two 3.5 mm Si(Li) detectors at 14 degr on either side
of the beam.
Two 56Fe self-supporting foil targets enriched to
99.95% were used. The areal densities were 0.69+-0.02
mg/cm2 and 1.63+-0.05 mg/cm2 with respective standard
deviation multiple scattering angles of +-0.10 degr.
and +-0.16 degr.
Small corrections were made due to analyser dead time
(<2%) and Faraday cup leakage current and cycling dead
The overall FWHM energy resolution obtained was between
100 to 120 keV.
